evals.prompt: We have 5 projects across web, iOS, and backend. I need a dashboard showing validation posture so the team lead can spot which ones need urgent attention before next sprint planning. Set it up.
evals.expected_output: User runs collect-team-metrics.sh, edits .vf/team/projects.json to add all 5 projects with paths, runs team-dashboard.sh, gets color-coded table with Critical/Attention/Healthy buckets. Dashboard shows scores, regressions, coverage (though coverage will be 0 until /validate-benchmark outputs enriched format).

evals.prompt: Our validation scores show project-alpha at 42%, but I don't know who's supposed to be fixing the failing journeys. Assign ownership so accountability is clear.
evals.expected_output: User runs assign-ownership.sh with --project project-alpha --journey login-flow --owner alice for each failing journey. assign-ownership.sh appends to .vf/team/ownership.json. Next team-dashboard.sh run shows ownership column in table.
